blockingIssues.description: The startup/env/logging fix still has no auditable commit traceability: the original handoff cites unrelated commit `b02aa24` and the rerun handoff omits `commitId` entirely, so the feature still lacks the required commit-backed audit trail.

Commit `ae5f99a51db5f16657fbbe62c64e3bf4469fc710` still adds unrelated exchanges/treasury/onchain VAL-EXT negative-path coverage in `tests/invalid-params.test.ts`, so the diff is not actually isolated to the assigned catalog/simple/search/global surfaces.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an escalation path for features whose acceptance criteria explicitly require commit traceability when the repository is already dirty, so workers do not end in a state where the code fix exists but no auditable commit can satisfy the feature contract.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The rerun review for `startup-env-logging-determinism-hardening` confirmed the worker followed dirty-tree guidance yet still could not satisfy the feature note requiring commit traceability for the env/logging change.

overrideJustification.reason: Remaining blockers are audit-only feature-traceability issues, not unmet runtime behavior or failing validators.
details: startup-env-logging-determinism-hardening fix is present and validated; blocker is missing/wrong commit traceability only.
catalog-simple-contract-regression-guards behavior and regression coverage are validated; blocker is commit scope pollution from unrelated VAL-EXT test coverage.
